sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 3

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 3:1
items.html:
Explaining the quarry of the soul
Now the matter of the quarry of the soul will be clarified — of which they said that she is "a part of God above," etc., and about which we asked above: how, and by what, does the form of the soul change from His simple Light so as to become distinguished thereby from the Whole? Now it is understood that a great disparity of form was indeed made in her. For although He includes every form that can be thought or contemplated, nevertheless, after what was said above, you find one form that is not included in Him — namely, the form of the desire to receive; for from whom, God forbid, would He receive? But the souls, whose entire creation is because He wished to delight them — this being the thought of creation, as above — must necessarily have this law imprinted in them: to want and to crave to receive His abundance. And in this they are distinguished from Him, for their form has been changed from His. For it has already been clarified that a corporeal essence is divided and separated by the force of motion and remoteness of place, while a spiritual essence is divided and separated by disparity of form; and according to the measure of the disparity of form between one and another, so is the distance between them measured. And if the disparity of form reaches actual oppositeness, from one extreme to the other, then a complete severance and separation is made, until they can no longer feed from one another, for they are considered strangers to each other.
